critical.mandate: Every deployment must have a verified rollback plan
All approval gates must be explicitly confirmed
Never deploy without pre-deployment checks passing

step.title: Load Deployment Context
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — understand system components
Read {planning_artifacts}/infrastructure-design.md if available
Read {test_artifacts}/traceability-matrix.md — verify requirement coverage
Read {test_artifacts}/ci-setup.md — verify CI quality gates
Read {planning_artifacts}/readiness-report.md — verify PASS status
Identify release scope: what stories, features, fixes are included

step.title: Optional: Accessibility Testing
step.ask: Would you like to create an accessibility test plan? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended for frontend/user-facing applications. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/testing/workflows/accessibility-testing/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: accessibility testing can be set up anytime later with /gaia-a11y-testing

step.title: Pre-Deployment Checks
step.action: Verify DB migrations are tested and reversible
Confirm feature flags are configured correctly
Check configuration changes are applied to target environment
Verify secrets rotation is complete if required

step.title: Dependency Verification
step.action: Confirm external service availability (APIs, databases, caches)
Verify API compatibility with consumers
Check third-party service SLAs and maintenance windows

step.title: Rollback Readiness
step.action: Verify rollback plan exists and is documented
Confirm rollback has been tested in staging
Verify DB migration rollback scripts work
Define rollback trigger criteria

step.title: Automated Verification Gates
step.action: Verify readiness-report.md status is PASS — if not PASS, HALT deployment
Verify traceability-matrix.md coverage is complete — flag gaps as blocking
Verify ci-setup.md quality gates are enforced (not advisory-only)

step.title: QA Sign-Off
step.ask: QA sign-off: confirm all acceptance tests pass and no critical bugs are open. Enter CONFIRMED or provide blocking issue.
step.check: HALT: QA sign-off is required before deployment. Resolve the blocking issue and re-run.

step.title: Security Sign-Off
step.ask: Security sign-off: confirm security review is complete with no critical findings. Enter CONFIRMED or provide blocking findings.
step.check: HALT: Security sign-off is required before deployment. Resolve blocking findings and re-run.

step.title: Stakeholder Approval
step.ask: Stakeholder approval: confirm release scope is approved. Enter CONFIRMED.
step.check: HALT: Stakeholder approval required before deployment.

step.title: Gap Analysis Gate

critical.mandate: This step implements FR-238 (E19-S19, ADR-030 §10.22). It enforces that a test gap analysis has been run for the current sprint before deployment is allowed. The gate is HALT-enforced when the gap analysis file is missing — it is not advisory. A bypass flag (`--skip-gap-gate`) exists for exceptional releases and must log a warning plus record the bypass in the checklist output. Critical gaps surface as WARNINGs only — they do not block deployment.

step.action: SKIP FLAG CHECK (AC4): Inspect the invocation arguments passed to /gaia-deploy-checklist. If the flag `--skip-gap-gate` is present: log the warning "gap gate bypassed by --skip-gap-gate" to the console, set a gap_gate_bypassed flag on the workflow state so Step 7 records the bypass in the generated checklist output (e.g., "Gap Analysis Gate: BYPASSED via --skip-gap-gate"), and proceed directly to Step 7 without performing the file check. This is the only path that skips the file check.

RESOLVE SPRINT WINDOW (AC2): Read {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml and extract the current sprint's `sprint_id` and `started` (ISO date YYYY-MM-DD) fields. These define the sprint window — from the sprint start date to today. If sprint-status.yaml does not exist, HALT with "Deploy blocked: sprint-status.yaml missing — cannot determine sprint window. Run /gaia-sprint-plan or pass --skip-gap-gate if intentional."

GAP ANALYSIS FILE CHECK (AC1, AC2, FR-238): Check {test_artifacts}/ (which resolves to docs/test-artifacts/ under the project root) for any file matching the pattern `test-gap-analysis-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md` where the date is within the sprint window (sprint_start_date ≤ date ≤ today, inclusive). Preferred match: `test-gap-analysis-{sprint_start_date}.md`. Any dated file within the window satisfies the gate. If one or more matching files exist, capture the newest one as {gap_file} and proceed to the CRITICAL GAP CHECK below. If no matching file exists, fall through to the FAIL path.

FAIL PATH (AC3): If no test-gap-analysis file exists within the sprint window, HALT with this exact message: "Deploy blocked: no gap analysis found for sprint {sprint_id}. Run `/gaia-test-gap-analysis` first." Do NOT advance to Step 7. The release manager must either run /gaia-test-gap-analysis or re-run with --skip-gap-gate to bypass the gate explicitly.

CRITICAL GAP CHECK (AC5): If {gap_file} exists, read its YAML frontmatter for a `gap_count` field and scan the "Gap Table" section of the markdown for rows with `severity: critical` (or a "critical" column value). Count the critical gaps. If one or more critical gaps are found: emit a WARNING (not a HALT) containing "WARNING: Gap analysis {gap_file} contains {N} CRITICAL gap(s):" followed by a bulleted list of each critical gap's description. Set a critical_gaps_warned flag on the workflow state so Step 7 includes the warning in the generated checklist output. Then proceed to Step 7. This is a non-blocking warning — the deployment continues.
PASS PATH: If {gap_file} exists and has no critical gaps, emit "Gap Analysis Gate: PASSED — gap analysis {gap_file} found, no critical gaps." and proceed to Step 7.

step.title: Generate Checklist
step.template-output: Generate deployment checklist with: pre-deployment checks, dependency status, rollback readiness, approval gates, go/no-go recommendation.
